title: [WIP] - Generate seccomp profile by tracing the syscalls made by the container
body:
This pull request adds the ability for podman to generate seccomp profiles by tracing the syscalls made by the container. The generated profile would whitelist all the syscalls made and blacklist every other syscall.

The syscalls are traced by launching a binary by using the `prestart` OCI-hook. The binary started spawns a child process which attaches function `enter_trace` to the `raw_syscalls:sys_enter` tracepoint using eBPF. The function looks at all the syscalls made on the system and writes the syscalls which have the same PID namespace as the container to the perf buffer. The perf buffer is read by the process in the userspace and generates a seccomp profile when the container exits.

There are a few limitations to this approach:
- Needs `CAP_SYS_ADMIN` to run
- Compiles C code on the fly 
- Cannot use `podman run --rm` along with this ability

To build it, we need extra dependencies namely `bcc-devel` and `kernel-headers` for Fedora and `bcc-tools` and `linux-headers-[..]` for Ubuntu.

Interface:
```bash
sudo podman run --annotation io.podman.trace-syscall=[absolute path to the json file] IMAGE COMMAND
```
The profile will be created at the path provided to the annotation.


***CIRRUS: TEST IMAGES***

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

/ok-to-test
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *weirdwiz* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> This commit adds the flag --genseccomp to podman which can trace the syscalls made by a container and create a container profile from all the syscalls collected

I think this should go under `podman generate`

> > This commit adds the flag --genseccomp to podman which can trace the syscalls made by a container and create a container profile from all the syscalls collected
> 
> I think this should go under `podman generate`

`podman generate` operates on running containers. I find it easier to just add a flag to `podman-run` and `podman-create`.

@rhatdan WDYT?

> @weirdwiz, we need docs and bash-completion as well before we can merge.

And tests :^)
@vrothberg Yes, I'm on it :D

> > > This commit adds the flag --genseccomp to podman which can trace the syscalls made by a container and create a container profile from all the syscalls collected
> > 
> > 
> > I think this should go under `podman generate`
> 
> `podman generate` operates on running containers. I find it easier to just add a flag to `podman-run` and `podman-create`.
> 
> @rhatdan WDYT?

I agree this needs to be set before running the container, so I don't think it should be in generate.

I'm somewhat inclined to say we should drop the flag and make it trigger on an environment variable in the container or similar - something we can document as part of the hook that won't require explicit intervention in Podman. We'd need to extend the hook to put the resulting profile in a better place, but I think that should be possible.

@weirdwiz, once a comment is fixed, please click on `Resolve Conversation`. This way it's easier to keep track of which comments are still valid. Once a conversation is resolved, the participants will be notified (e..g, via email) and can check.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3573) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@weirdwiz Needs a rebase.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3466) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
/retest

> The gating test fails because the packages are not installed in the image, is there a way to fix that?

Hmmm, not sure about gating, that runs in a container and appears to work fine.  From [the most recent results](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6286389986983936) it looks like remote-client testing in F30 and Ubuntu18  fail in new tests with:

```
[+1175s] ls: .: Operation not permitted
[+1175s] output: ls: .: Operation not permitted
```

The other error - failure to compile due to missing ``<bcc/bcc_common.h>`` seem very odd, given other VMs using the same images did not have this problem.  The only thing I can think of is maybe the tasks are running against different images (maybe one from a re-run of the `test_build_cache_images`?).

I'll fire up some VMs manually based on the latest images and confirm what I'm able...

```
[+0711s] ==> Builds finished. The artifacts of successful builds are:
[+0711s] --> fedora-30: A disk image was created: fedora-30-libpod-6286389986983936
[+0711s] --> fedora-30: 
[+0711s] --> xfedora-30: A disk image was created: xfedora-30-libpod-6286389986983936
[+0711s] --> xfedora-30: 
[+0711s] --> ubuntu-18: A disk image was created: ubuntu-18-libpod-6286389986983936
[+0711s] --> ubuntu-18: 
[+0711s] --> fedora-29: A disk image was created: fedora-29-libpod-6286389986983936
[+0711s] --> fedora-29: 
```

Oops, my bad.  I see now, ALL the F29 images failed.  I started up VMs to check:

* `fedora-30-libpod-6286389986983936` has `/usr/include/bcc/bcc_common.h`
* `xfedora-30-libpod-6286389986983936` (for testing crun + cgroups v2) has `/usr/include/bcc/bcc_common.h`
*  `ubuntu-18-libpod-6286389986983936` has `/usr/include/bcc/bcc_common.h`
* `fedora-29-libpod-6286389986983936` does not

So I think the only case you need to solve for is F29.  I know sometimes `dnf` doesn't actually fail if a package is missing.  Perhaps try (temporarily) removing the `ooe.sh` from the start of `sudo dnf install -y...`.  Then you'll see all the output when it builds the images.

Unless, maybe bcc-devel is simply not available for F29?

If that's the case, then both the tests and makefile will need conditionals when the host doesn't have it (ugg) :confounded:

> If that's the case, then both the tests and makefile will need conditionals when the host doesn't have it 

I added conditionals in the Makefile and the tests which checked if `libbcc` was present on the system, that passes for the F29 systems because `libbcc` get's installed, but the package in F29 repos is old and doesn't ship with the required headers.

> the package in F29 repos is old and doesn't ship with the required headers.

Blast, that's too bad.  So is libbcc on F29 simply too old to be used for your purpose?

It won't be too long (hopefully a handful of months maybe?) before we can drop F29 (when F31 comes out).  Until then it sounds like this PR needs to fence-off F29 in the new test (There's a os and os-version check helper function in utils.go IIRC).
I think we should drop support for F29 now. Is it too early, IE No images for F31?  Would love to be testing on it ASAP.

> Blast, that's too bad. So is libbcc on F29 simply too old to be used for your purpose?

Yes, on F30 it's `0.10.0-1` and on F29 it's `0.8.0-1`, the older version has different header files.

@rhatdan opened https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3901 in eager anticipation - though, happily, it seems [we have a development build of cloud-images already!](https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/fedora/linux/development/31/Cloud/x86_64/images/).  I can start working on any kinks from that, so when it releases I only need to update a few URLs.
@weirdwiz, @cevich, is there a way we can prevent `libbcc` from being installed on F29? It would be great to unblock testing this PR.

The system-test failure is #3870 so don't worry about that one.

```
[+0099s] # Error: error removing containers [e3d3a6aef941848214061073e9ddb1deee484d1c8784df8cb006158400f8f810] for image "ddbd497a49098deaa328ff9f58e7098184237c8d74fd43082c8678855c0fffcf": could not remove container "e3d3a6aef941848214061073e9ddb1deee484d1c8784df8cb006158400f8f810": remove /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/e3d3a6aef941848214061073e9ddb1deee484d1c8784df8cb006158400f8f810/userdata/shm: device or resource busy
[+0099s] # [ rc=2 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
[+0099s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
[+0099s] # #| FAIL: exit code is 2; expected 0
[+0099s] # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
[+0099s] # $ ./bin/podman-remote rm -a -f
[+0099s] # $ ./bin/podman-remote rmi -f build_test
```

> is there a way we can prevent libbcc from being installed on F29? It would be great to unblock testing this PR.

Yes this is possible with a condition in `fedora_setup.sh`; however, similar conditionals also need to be added to all affected tests.  IIRC, it installs okay on F29, so perhaps only the tests should not run on F29 (we have a utilities module with functions to check for OS and OS version)?  That would be easier than maintaining two conditionals.  Also because F29 will be going away soon-ish.

> Yes this is possible with a condition in fedora_setup.sh; however, similar conditionals also need to be added to all affected tests.

I have added the condition to `fedora_setup.sh` in the previous commit. 

>  IIRC, it installs okay on F29, so perhaps only the tests should not run on F29 (we have a utilities module with functions to check for OS and OS version)? 

The tests don't run when the hooks aren't installed. And the hooks don't install if the library is not present.
Excellent :smiley:
Do fedora 31 images exists yet?  IE Can we switch 29 to 31?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3948) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Restarted the failing jobs.

You need to add this patch to get make install to work.
[install.patch.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/3585214/install.patch.txt)

After playing around with this for a while, I would also suggest the following patch.

[trace.patch.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/3585424/trace.patch.txt)

Sorting the syscalls makes it easier to view and do a diff between newer traces and older ones.

Don't hard code paths into your executable.
And lets not hard code podman into the annotation, since I might want to use this plugin with other tools.

> Do fedora 31 images exists yet? IE Can we switch 29 to 31?

@rhatdan a VM image exists and boots (`fedora-31-libpod-5654728358232064`) however it fails testing (somewhat expected) and is based on a pre-release base-image (so, not safe for general consumption yet).  Ref: #3901
What's going on with the tests?
@cevich, do you know what's going on in the tests? I've never seen those errors before.
/test images
@weirdwiz Could you rebase this PR and repush it.

```
[[ "$PACKER_BUILDER_NAME" == "xfedora-30" ]] || \
    '$SCRIPT_BASE/build_release.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
/tmp/scripts71c483f15fb90badb37c5821b6d95526.sh: line 6: $SCRIPT_BASE/build_release.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP}: No such file or directory
```
Restarted the (flaked) tests. Good job resolving the package issues, @weirdwiz !
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3754) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Closing the PR as we created a dedicated project for the OCI hooks: https://github.com/containers/oci-seccomp-bpf-hook/

Thank you so much, @weirdwiz, for your great work! Looking forward to continue working with you over at https://github.com/containers/oci-seccomp-bpf-hook/ :rocket:
Thanks for the help and mentorship @vrothberg @rhatdan, wouldn't have been possible without it!
